message:
perf: reduce allocations in SSE parsing, session writes, agent init, and auth locking

Four targeted performance improvements across hot paths:

1. SSE parser (src/sse.rs):
   - Replace String with Cow<'static, str> for SseEvent.event field to
     avoid heap-allocating the ubiquitous "message" default on every event
   - Pre-allocate Vec::with_capacity(4) for parsed events batch
   - Replace BOM-strip copy (.strip_prefix().to_string()) with in-place
     buffer.drain(..3) to avoid reallocating the entire stream buffer

2. Session serialization (src/session.rs):
   - Replace strategy of building a full String in memory then writing it
     all at once via fs::write, with streaming serde_json::to_writer
     through a BufWriter directly to the temp file
   - Eliminates peak memory spike for large sessions with many entries
     since entries are serialized one at a time instead of concatenated

3. Agent message buffer (src/agent.rs):
   - Pre-allocate Vec::with_capacity(prompts.len() + 8) instead of
     Vec::new() since we know the minimum number of messages upfront

4. Auth file locking (src/auth.rs):
   - Replace fixed 50ms sleep polling with exponential backoff + jitter
   - Starts at 10ms base, doubles per attempt (capped at 500ms), adds
     jitter derived from elapsed nanoseconds to avoid thundering herd
   - Applied to both sync lock_file() and async lock_file_async()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
